title: IHC-Amplified Fluorescent Frozen Sections
author: Elizabeth Smith
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8rmhv46
materials: blocking buffer (10% normal donkey serum in 1.0%)

steps.title: Day 1
steps.instruction: Day 1: Using the PAP Pen, carefully draw a water barrier circle around the tissue sections on the slide – allow this circle to dry for several seconds or up to approx. one minute

steps.title: Rinse slides with PBS (pH 7.3-7.4)
steps.instruction: Rinse slides with PBS (pH 7.3-7.4): 4 x 5 min each

steps.title: Rinse slides with 0
steps.instruction: Rinse slides with 0.5% BSA + 0.4% Triton X-100 in PBS): 1 x 10 min

steps.title: Remove slides one at a time...
steps.instruction: Remove slides one at a time and using a clean Kimwipe, carefully wipe around the tissue sections to dry the slide

steps.title: Place the slides into a black,...
steps.instruction: Place the slides into a black, covered slide incubation box/humidity box

steps.title: Cover the tissue sections with blocking...
steps.instruction: Cover the tissue sections with blocking buffer (10% normal donkey serum in 1.0% BSA + 0.4% Triton X-100 + PBS)

steps.title: Allow the sections to remain in blocking buffer for 1
steps.instruction: Allow the sections to remain in blocking buffer for 1.5-2 hrs. at RT

steps.title: Pour off the blocking buffer
steps.instruction: Pour off the blocking buffer

steps.title: Replace with primary antibody solution (antibody...
steps.instruction: Replace with primary antibody solution (antibody of choice diluted in 1.0% BSA + 0.4% Triton X-100 + PBS)

steps.title: Incubate tissue with primary antibody overnight in incubation box
steps.instruction: Incubate tissue with primary antibody overnight in incubation box

steps.title: Day 2
steps.instruction: Day 2: Rinse slides with PBS: 4 x 5 min

steps.title: Rinse slides with 0
steps.instruction: Rinse slides with 0.5% BSA + 0.4% Triton X-100 + PBS: 1 x 10 min

steps.title: Place the slides into a black,...
steps.instruction: Place the slides into a black, covered slide incubation box/humidity box

steps.title: Cover the tissue sections with blocking...
steps.instruction: Cover the tissue sections with blocking buffer (10% normal donkey serum in 1.0% BSA + 0.4% Triton X-100 + PBS)

steps.title: Allow the sections to remain in blocking buffer for 1
steps.instruction: Allow the sections to remain in blocking buffer for 1.5-2 hrs. at RT

steps.title: Prepare fluorescent secondary antibody (secondary antibody...
steps.instruction: Prepare fluorescent secondary antibody (secondary antibody should be diluted in 1.0% BSA + 0.4% Triton X-100 + PBS)

steps.title: Cover the tissue with the secondary...
steps.instruction: Cover the tissue with the secondary antibody solution and incubate for 2 hrs. at RT in the incubation box. *From this point on, use low light and/or cover tissues.*

steps.title: Rinse slides with PBS
steps.instruction: Rinse slides with PBS: 4 x 5 min

steps.title: Remove excess PBS with a Kimwipe
steps.instruction: Remove excess PBS with a Kimwipe

steps.title: Carefully add a drop of mounting...
steps.instruction: Carefully add a drop of mounting medium to the center of the tissue and apply cover glass

steps.title: Seal cover glass with clear nail polish
steps.instruction: Seal cover glass with clear nail polish. For thicker tissue, add a weight before sealing

steps.title: Prepare fluorescent Streptavidin secondary antibody (should be diluted in 1
steps.instruction: Prepare fluorescent Streptavidin secondary antibody (should be diluted in 1.0% BSA + 0.4% Triton X-100 + PBS)

steps.title: Cover the tissue with the fluorescent...
steps.instruction: Cover the tissue with the fluorescent Streptavidin antibody solution and incubate for 2 hrs. at RT in the incubation box.

steps.title: Rinse slides with PBS:4 x 5 min
steps.instruction: Rinse slides with PBS:4 x 5 min

steps.title: Remove excess PBS with a Kimwipe
steps.instruction: Remove excess PBS with a Kimwipe
